comment.body:
Thank you for responding again, this leads into another question then about all of this: 

What flows actually matter then? If MM hedging flows are pretty minuscule from what you’re saying (for the most part or maybe in interpreting this wrong) what flows actually matter in terms of importance? Spot buying of the individual names that make up the basket of SPX? Futures flows? Something else? 

I’m trying to understand what really actually matter at the FUNDAMENTAL level. I’m realizing the options market and math behind it is complex (work in progress for understanding and application) but I realized I don’t know what ACTUALLY matters when building my own models, and no one seems to talk about what really matters just how mechanics works for xyz Greek and product (not you but other furu’s). What REALLY measures supply and demand accurately, then building models (in my mind and computationally) on top of that fundamental layer

comment.body:
market is driven by supply and demand.
the aggregated supply and demand all over the markets.
thats it.
don't look for free lunch, it doesn't exist.
options market has the highest pre-projectable impact, and this instrument simultanously bets on spot, time and volatility, influencing correlations, and the distribution.
thats it.
